flashcards.Back: An internal database on a switch that maps hardware MAC addresses to physical ports. it is used to make intelligent forwarding decisions.
Content Addressable Memory table. This is the hardware implementation of the MAC address table, optimized for extremely fast lookups.
The process by which a switch populates its address table by examining the source MAC address of every incoming frame.
A mechanism that removes entries from the MAC table if they have been inactive for a specific period (default 300 seconds), keeping the table current.
The action of sending an Ethernet frame out a specific port when the destination MAC address is found in the switch's address table.
A switch decision to drop a frame instead of forwarding it, occurring when the destination MAC is on the same port where the frame was received.
The process of sending a frame out all active ports except the incoming port. this happens for unknown unicast, broadcast, and multicast traffic.
A frame with a destination MAC address that is not currently in the switch's MAC table. The switch must flood this frame to ensure delivery.
A frame destined for all devices on a local segment, identified by the MAC address FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF. it is always flooded by switches.
The logical boundary within which a broadcast frame can travel. Routers and VLANs are used to define and limit these domains.
A physical segment where packets can collide. Modern switches create a separate collision domain for every port, eliminating collisions in full-duplex mode.
A switching method where the entire frame is received and checked for errors (FCS) before it is forwarded. This ensures only valid frames are sent.
A low-latency switching method where forwarding begins as soon as the destination MAC is read, without waiting for the rest of the frame or error checking.
A hybrid method that waits for the first 64 bytes of a frame (the minimum Ethernet size) before forwarding, filtering out most collision-related runts.
A 4-byte CRC value at the end of an Ethernet frame used for error detection. The receiver recalculates this value to verify data integrity.
The Protocol Data Unit at the data link layer, which is called a 'Frame'. it contains the MAC addresses and the payload from higher layers.
Application-Specific Integrated Circuit. Specialized hardware chips in a switch that perform frame processing and forwarding at 'wirespeed'.
A communication mode allowing simultaneous transmit and receive on a switch port, effectively doubling bandwidth and eliminating collisions.
A legacy mode where a device can either transmit or receive, but not both at once. it requires CSMA/CD to manage collisions.
A feature that restricts which MAC addresses are allowed to access a port, providing a basic but effective form of network access control.
The physical interface on a switch where a frame enters the device. The switch uses this port to learn the source MAC address.
The physical interface on a switch where a frame leaves the device, determined by the destination MAC address lookup in the CAM table.
A frame intended for a single specific destination device, identified by a unique hardware MAC address.
A frame destined for a group of devices. it is identified by a special MAC address prefix (e.g., 01:00:5E for IPv4 multicast).
Ternary Content Addressable Memory. A specialized memory type used in multilayer switches for high-speed lookups of ACLs and QoS policies.
The time delay between when a frame enters a switch port and when it begins to exit the destination port.
Internal memory used to temporarily store frames when the egress port is busy, preventing data loss during traffic bursts.
The practice of using a switch to provide dedicated bandwidth to every device, effectively reducing the size of collision domains to just two devices (host and switch).
Maximum Transmission Unit. The largest frame size allowed on an Ethernet segment, typically 1518 bytes including the header and FCS.
An 8-byte identifier used in spanning tree to elect a root bridge, consisting of a priority value and the switch's MAC address.
